name: Lumpia (Philippine Egg Rolls from Scratch)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the batter: In a large bowl, combine flour, water (minus 2 tbsp), and salt. Mix thoroughly with a wire whisk until no lumps remain. If the mixture is too thick, add a little more water. It should be similar to crepe batter. If it thickens as it sits, you may need to add more water.
2. Cook the batter: Heat a 6-inch non-stick skillet and lightly grease with oil. Pour in enough batter to cover the bottom of the pan. Cook over low heat until the edges curl away from the sides (about 5 minutes). Remove from heat and set aside. Repeat, using fresh oil each time.
3. Prepare the filling: In a large skillet, cook ground pork or beef until no pink remains. Drain excess fat. Add shrimp, chopped onions, water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, celery, garlic, and carrot. Cook until carrot is almost tender. Stir in remaining ingredients except egg white and oil. Cook until bean sprouts are limp.
4. Assemble the rolls: Drain excess liquid from the filling mixture. Place a few teaspoons of filling near one edge of each batter circle. Roll up tightly, sealing with a dab of egg white at the end.
5. Fry the rolls: Heat corn oil in a deep skillet to about 375°F (190°C). Fry rolls for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Drain on paper towels before serving.
6. Prepare the sauce: Combine all ingredients except cornstarch in a bowl, then stir in cornstarch mixed with some of the pineapple juice mixture until smooth. Return to pot and simmer until thickened.
Note: If making ahead of time, refrigerate the rolls and reheat before frying.
